channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Rolling Applications Accepting Students for CSTEP!
item.description: The Collegiate Science & Technology Entry Program (CSTEP) is excited to announce that applications for the Spring semester are now open! CSTEP is an academic enrichment program designed to support New York State residents who are economically disadvantaged and pursuing careers in STEM or health-related fields.
item.author: Students

item.title: Poster Review with the Success Center’s Writing Lab
item.description: If you’re preparing for the Undergraduate Research Symposium or an upcoming conference, join us for a Poster Review Pizza Night hosted by the Writing Lab! Bring your poster draft and enjoy pizza while receiving personalized feedback on your visual design, content clarity, and overall presentation. Tuesday, April 14th 5-6pm Success Center: Classroom 107, Folsom Library
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Review Sessions: Week of 4/12-4/18
item.description: Week of 4/12- 4/18: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! Thermodynamics: Sunday, 4/12 (5-7pm) at the Success Center Essence of Eng. Mechanics: Monday, 4/13 (5-7pm) at the Success Center Computer Science: Tuesday, 4/14 (7-9pm) with SASE in the Nason Classroom Diff Eq.: Wednesday, 4/15 (7-9pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: Fireside Chat with CJ Winslow M.Eng ’06, B.S. ’05
item.description: Date: Monday, April 13, 2026 5:00 PM - 7:00 PM - Design it. Build it. Grow it. Discover how CJ Winslow turned engineering, innovation, and manufacturing into a thriving company. Learn how technical skills, product development, and smart risk-taking can transform an engineer into a business leader. RSVP https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1USf7r28Dpbs63x_jYcjh_bG8qZh1X9fZ1vn-uH-ZSsk/edit?usp=sharing 4/13/26 5:00pm - 7:00pm in the Union Theater
item.author: Students

item.title: NYS Dept. of Transportation Info Session
item.description: Tuesday, April 14th 20265:30 pm - 8:00 pmHosted at the Center for Career and Professional Development - DCC 209 - Darrin Communications Center, Darrin Communications Center Troy, New York, United StatesHosted by the NYS Department of TransportationRegister here: https://rpi.joinhandshake.com/events/1869924/share_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: Computer Science Colloquia - Vihan Shah
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Vihan Shah Marie Curie Fellow at University of Birmingham Huge Network, Tiny Resources: Algorithms for Massive Graphs In this talk, I will discuss how such models provide new ways to process massive graphs. In particular, I will focus on two closely related directions: streaming algorithms and sublinear-time algorithms.
item.author: Students

item.title: Bird & Brine Delayed Opening April 10, 2026
item.description: Bird N’ Brine, located in the Student Union, will have a delayed opening on Friday, April 10, 2026, due to scheduled equipment maintenance. Please stay tuned for updates regarding our opening time that day by following Rensselaer Dining on social media @rensselaerdining
item.author: Community

item.title: Intellectual Property & Technology Licensing Seminar
item.description: Are you working on groundbreaking research? Curious about how to protect, commercialize, or launch a start-up based on your innovations through IP? Join us on April 14th for an exciting campuswide seminar hosted by the Office of Intellectual Property and Technology Licensing (IPTL) at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (RPI)! This seminar is your chance to learn how IPTL supports faculty and students in translating research into real-world impact through patents, licensing, or industry…
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Chemistry Seminar Series
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 12:00 AM - 1:00 AM - The Harteck Lecture Series in Physical Chemistry will feature guest speaker Prof. Dimitrios Pantazis, Max Planck Institute for Coal Research, who will speak on the topic Molecular Principles of Solar Energy Conversion in Photosynthesis.
item.author: Community

item.title: The Design Showcase Welcomes Guest Speaker, Brent Brunell ’94 from GE Vernova
item.description: Brent Brunell, ’94, the Technology Director of Digital & Electrical Systems at GE Vernova Advanced Research, will visit campus as a featured guest speaker for the Design Lab Showcase on Wednesday, April 22, 2026. Brent’s keynote presentation beginning at 3:00 PM in DCC 324.
item.author: Community

item.title: Design Lab Showcase to be held Wednesday, April 22nd, 2026 from 1:00 - 4:00 p.m.
item.description: The Design Showcase will be held on Wednesday, Wednesday, April 22nd, 2026 from 1:00 - 4:00 p.m. in the DCC Great Hall. Students from multiple engineering disciplines (Computer & Systems, Electrical, Industrial & Management, Materials and Mechanical Engineering) solve real-world design challenges for industry, entrepreneurs and academic/medical partners. Visit the showcase to: Interact with project teams and view their achievements. Learn how students solve complex design challenges. Get…
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday April 17: Webex Event Training
item.description: Hosting a campus event or supporting a student webinar? Join a hands-on Webex Event Training Webinar, part of the MMS Collaboration Software Training series. Register now for this webinar presented via Webex on Friday April 17 from 11am-12pm: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700 Learn the basics of Webex Event hosting, with demonstration and Q&A, along with new features recently added to the hosting platform.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Debate Public Debate
item.description: The RPI Debate Team will be presenting a double-header public debate on Thursday, April 16 beginning at 6:30pm in Sage 3303. The first debate topic will be increasing nuclear energy production in the United States. The second debate topic will be censorship of social media. This event is open to all students, faculty, and staff.
item.author: Community

item.title: Faculty/Staff Memorial Service to be Held April 14
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 11:00 AM - 1:00 PM - Faculty/Staff Memorial Service to be Held Wednesday, April 14, 2026 11:00 AM - 1:00 PM. In remembrance of the individuals we lost this past year, the Pillars of Rensselaer invite all members of the Rensselaer community, including alumni and friends, to attend this year’s Faculty and Staff Memorial Service, which will be held at the Chapel + Cultural Center, located at 2125 Burdett Avenue, Troy.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI at Work Hosts Information Session on April 14
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 7:00 PM - 7:30 PM - RPI at Work Dean Aric Krause, Ph. D., will be reviewing the online, project-based graduate degrees and certificates. Expect to hear details about how RPI Instructor Mentors provide industry expertise, advice, and mentorship in a targeted and experiential learning environment. Please RSVP below if you plan to attend and learn more about RPI at Work.
item.author: Community

item.title: Faculty & Staff Appreciation Lunch - April 22nd, 2026
item.description: Join us on Wednesday April 22nd for a $6 (plus tax) lunch at Commons or Russell Sage Dining Halls as we get towards the end of another academic school year! We hope you can take a break, connect with colleagues, and enjoy this time together.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Rensselaer Faculty and Staff In Person Workshop - Reviewing Supported Whiteboarding Tools On Campus for Courses or Meetings
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - In this workshop we will take a look at two DotCIO supported whiteboarding tools available on campus. The first, is working with the whiteboard feature in Webex. The second tool is in Box, called Canvas. This session will offer an overview on these two similar tools giving you an opportunity to learn a little more about them and potentially consider using them in your classroom or meeting space. Please register for this session by 4/10 using…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Volunteers Needed: Late Night Breakfast
item.description:
We’re looking for volunteers on April 30th from 8:30 pm - 11 pm to help make Late Night Breakfast a success! Volunteers will assist with greeting students, serving food and helping to keep the good vibes going! If you’re interested in helping out or would like more information please email auxiliaryservices@rpi.edu. We’d love to have you on the team!

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
